title: Remote Access MFA Evidence Correlation ‚Äî VPN Success Without Step-Up Token

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 8.5.1 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 8.5.1 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.53: Remote Access MFA Evidence Correlation ‚Äî VPN Success Without Step-Up Token.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.53' running on index=netfw pan:globalprotect; index=o365 azure:aad:signin, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) for AAD sign-ins
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" subtype="gateway-connected" earliest=-24h
| eval session=coalesce(session_id,gp_session_id)
| eval user=lower(user)
| join type=left max=1 user [
    search index=o365 sourcetype="azure:aad:signin" earliest=-24h
    | eval user=lower(userPrincipalName)
    | where authenticationRequirement="multiFactorAuthentication" OR mfaDetail!="[]"
    | stats latest(_time) as mfa_time by user
  ]
| where isnull(mfa_time) OR mfa_time > _time + 300
| stats count by user, src, portal
| sort - count
description: Joins VPN authentication logs with identity provider MFA success events to prove remote entry into the CDE used multi-factor authentication for each session where policy requires it.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes netfw, o365 and sourcetypes azure:aad:signin, pan:globalprotect. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Clock-skew normalize `_time` fields across sources. (2) Tune join window; `mfa_time` should precede VPN connect. (3) Replace logic if Okta/other IdP. (4) Document compensating SAML MFA at IdP only. Indexes required: `index=netfw`, `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=azure:aad:signin`, `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`.
visualization: Table (sessions missing MFA join), timeline, single value (violations).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) for AAD sign-ins.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=netfw` `pan:globalprotect`; `index=o365` `azure:aad:signin`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Clock-skew normalize `_time` fields across sources. (2) Tune join window; `mfa_time` should precede VPN connect. (3) Replace logic if Okta/other IdP. (4) Document compensating SAML MFA at IdP only.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" subtype="gateway-connected" earliest=-24h
| eval session=coalesce(session_id,gp_session_id)
| eval user=lower(user)
| join type=left max=1 user [
    search index=o365 sourcetype="azure:aad:signin" earliest=-24h
    | eval user=lower(userPrincipalName)
    | where authenticationRequirement="multiFactorAuthentication" OR mfaDetail!="[]"
    | stats latest(_time) as mfa_time by user
  ]
| where isnull(mfa_time) OR mfa_time > _time + 300
| stats count by user, src, portal
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Remote Access MFA Evidence Correlation ‚Äî VPN Success Without Step-Up Token (PCI DSS Req 8.4.2, 8.5.1)** ‚Äî Joins VPN authentication logs with identity provider MFA success events to prove remote entry into the CDE used multi-factor authentication for each session where policy requires it.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `pan:globalprotect`; `index=o365` `azure:aad:signin`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) for AAD sign-ins. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: netfw; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=netfw, sourcetype="pan:globalprotect", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **session** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **user** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(mfa_time) OR mfa_time > _time + 300` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` aggregates events by **user, src, portal**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Remote Access MFA Evidence Correlation ‚Äî VPN Success Without Step-Up Token (PCI DSS Req 8.4.2, 8.5.1)** ‚Äî Joins VPN authentication logs with identity provider MFA success events to prove remote entry into the CDE used multi-factor authentication for each session where policy requires it.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `pan:globalprotect`; `index=o365` `azure:aad:signin`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) for AAD sign-ins. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: mfa_time, portal, session, src, user.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sessions missing MFA join), timeline, single value (violations).
grandmaExplanation: We joins VPN authentication logs with identity provider MFA success events to prove remote entry into the CDE used multi-factor authentication for each session where policy requires it. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Authentication (CIM data model).
